methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Ultra Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Max Friefeld that has raised $3M+. The valuation analysis draws on a sector universe of 12 robotics and manufacturing peers, including one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private firms such as 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and provides the primary valuation signal. Using total raised ($3M) as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $8M. A Peer-Median approach was not feasible; while 12 same-sector peers exist, the dataset contained zero same-stage comparables, one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—insufficient for reliable triangulation below the three-company threshold.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, centered on $8M. This range reflects 15% downside and upside variance around the stage-progression midpoint. Key uncertainties include the absence of traction metrics (revenue, user growth, technical milestones) and limited peer granularity at the Seed stage, which constrains confidence in the multiplier assumption. Any future valuation exercise should incorporate operational metrics and, if available, recent rounds from narrower robotics comparables to tighten the range.
